categories.name: .NET
categories.description: Code-quality and dependency-hygiene tools for .NET projects.

categories.name: Workspace
categories.description: Repository-level tools independent of any specific language.

tools.name: analyze_csharp_code
tools.description: Analyzes a C# source file for style, naming, async/await patterns, nullable safety, and structural issues.

tasks.name: analyze_csharp_async_patterns
tasks.description: Enforces async/await rules from dotnet-async-await.instructions.md: no async void (except event handlers), public async APIs require a CancellationToken parameter, and await expressions in non-test code use .ConfigureAwait(false).

tasks.name: analyze_csharp_coding_style
tasks.description: Enforces code-style rules from lang-csharp.instructions.md: no regions or decorative comments, mandatory braces, blank lines before control flow, expression-body arrow placement, XML doc comments on public and protected members, System.* using ordering, and expression-body style for methods and properties.

tasks.name: analyze_csharp_member_ordering
tasks.description: Enforces type member ordering from lang-csharp.instructions.md: by member kind, then by access level, then static-before-instance.

tasks.name: analyze_csharp_naming_conventions
tasks.description: Enforces naming conventions from lang-csharp.instructions.md and dotnet-coding-standards.instructions.md: I prefix on interfaces, Extensions suffix on extension classes, Async suffix on async methods, _camelCase for private instance fields, PascalCase for types, methods, properties, and events, and camelCase for parameters.

tasks.name: analyze_csharp_nullable_context
tasks.description: Enforces nullable-safety rules from lang-csharp.instructions.md: no #nullable disable directives, and no use of the null-forgiving (!) operator to suppress warnings.

tasks.name: analyze_csharp_project_structure
tasks.description: Enforces project-structure rules from dotnet-coding-standards.instructions.md: file-scoped namespaces, single type per file, file name matches type name, and no #pragma warning disable.

tasks.name: analyze_csharp_test_style
tasks.description: Enforces test-style rules from testing.instructions.md and dotnet-xunit.instructions.md: test class and method naming, no XML doc comments, Assert.Multiple for multi-assertion tests, no ConfigureAwait, and structure mirroring (file name, namespace) against the production project.

tools.name: analyze_nuget_references
tools.description: Analyzes NuGet package references for hygiene issues.

tasks.name: analyze_nuget_hygiene
tasks.description: Enforces NuGet hygiene rules from dotnet-nuget.instructions.md: no duplicate PackageReference entries, no floating or wildcard versions, no PackageReference without a Version attribute (unless Central Package Management is in use), and flags packages that have a built-in .NET equivalent.

tools.name: analyze_git_commit_message
tools.description: Validates a Git commit message against project conventions for format and content.

tasks.name: analyze_git_commit_format
tasks.description: Enforces commit-format rules from git-commit.instructions.md: Conventional Commits structure (type, scope, subject) and line-length limits.

tasks.name: analyze_git_commit_content
tasks.description: Enforces commit-content rules from git-commit.instructions.md: detects anti-patterns in the message body — vague summaries, repeated bullets, change-log spam, and similar low-signal content.

tools.name: read_editorconfig
tools.description: Resolves the effective .editorconfig properties that apply to a specific file.

tasks.name: get_editorconfig_rules
tasks.description: Resolves effective .editorconfig properties for a given file path by walking the directory tree, evaluating glob patterns, and applying section cascading.

tools.name: analyze_typescript_code
tools.description: Analyzes a TypeScript source file for coding-style issues.

tasks.name: analyze_typescript_coding_style
tasks.description: Enforces TypeScript coding-style rules from lang-typescript.instructions.md: no any, no enums, no @ts-ignore, no type assertions, no non-null assertions, no unconstrained generics, and no Function/Object/{} types.
